sections.paragraph:
ἐννόμως adv. (s. prec. entry; =in accordance w. law: Lysias 9, 12; 30, 35; Lucian, Symp. 32; Cass. Dio 56, 7, 2; Pr 31:25; Just., D. 67, 2 and 4) Ro 2:12 v.l. as substitute for and in the sense of ἐν νόμῳ subject to or in possession of the law (opp. ἀνόμως, which prob. gave rise to the v.l.).

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

prec. prec. = preceding

Lysias Lysias , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

opp. opp. = opposed to, opposite

prob. prob. = probable, probably

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 338
